title: Advanced Verb Agreement
description: Master the full subject-verb agreement paradigm across all persons and numbers.
learning_objectives: Apply correct verb forms for all 6 subject pronouns
Distinguish singular (S-form) from plural (base form) verbs
Build grammatically correct sentences with any subject-verb combination

intro_content.title: The Full Agreement Table
intro_content.explanation:
Now let's put it all together. For every subject, you need the right verb form:

• I/You/We/They → base form (grab, eat, want)
• He/She → S-form (grabs, eats, wants)

This is the complete present tense agreement system.

challenges.prompt_spoken: Build the sentence: They grab ball.
challenges.context_description: A group of children reaching for a ball
challenges.target_concept: Plural Subject with Base Form Verb
challenges.target_concept_explanation: 'They' is 3rd person PLURAL. Plural subjects always use the base verb form.
hints: 'They' refers to multiple people. Which gesture represents a group?
Plural subjects use BASE form: 'grab' not 'grabs'.
What do they grab? Add the object.

SUBJECT_THEY|GRABS|BALL.feedback: 'They' is PLURAL — use 'grab' (base form), not 'grabs' (S-form). Only He/She/It uses the S-form.
SUBJECT_THEY|GRABS|BALL.rule_reference: Plural subjects → base form. Singular 3rd person → S-form.

challenges.prompt_spoken: Build the sentence: You want apple.
challenges.context_description: Pointing at someone who looks at an apple
challenges.target_concept_explanation: 'You' is 2nd person — always uses the base verb form, whether singular or plural.
hints: 'You' is 2nd person. Which gesture points to the other person?
'You' always uses the base form: 'want' not 'wants'.

SUBJECT_YOU|WANTS|APPLE.feedback: 'You' uses the base form 'want', not 'wants'. The S-form is only for He/She/It.

key_takeaways: I/You/We/They → base form (grab, eat, want, stop, go, drink)
He/She → S-form (grabs, eats, wants, stops, goes, drinks)
This rule only applies in PRESENT tense
Practice all combinations until they feel automatic
common_mistakes: Using S-form with They (They grabs ✗ → They grab ✓)
Using S-form with You (You wants ✗ → You want ✓)
Forgetting S-form with He/She (He grab ✗ → He grabs ✓)
